question.text: Which of the following types of mutual fund schemes can a new cadre distributor (such as a retired bank official) sell?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** New cadre distributors (postal agents, retired government/bank officials with 10+ years of service) are allowed to sell only specific low-risk or simple product categories. Liquid schemes are one of the five permitted categories. **The Trap:** Sectoral, small-cap, and credit risk funds are more complex and risky products — these are NOT in the permitted list for new cadre distributors. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under regulatory framework. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember the five permitted categories: Liquid/Money Market, Index, Diversified Equity, Retirement Benefit, and Fixed Maturity Plans (FMPs). If a product does not fall into these five, you cannot sell it.
